finding_results.notes: Kai reaches Tomas in six seconds and explicitly observes that the blackout is still active. Tomas does not coherently return until the twelfth second.
The chapter now explains why Kai has access to the traffic log, what visual marker flags the transfers as unauthorized, and why ordinary athlete view would have hidden the routing data before.
Yusuf still admits theft and motive, but the confession uses new syntax and pressure specific to this confrontation.
The wrist ache keeps the cold-air marrow pressure and removes the repeated healing backstory.
Kai's calibration-bay entry now assumes prior knowledge of the virus and contract, and Yusuf references Farah without reteaching her condition.
Tomas's post-crash speech is rendered as small, careful, and narrowed under stress, while Kai retains the chapter's intentional flatness cue.
Petra's entrance is now described through speed and medical action rather than hair color.
Chapter-wide punctuation was restructured to remove expository and appositional em-dashes. Verified count after revision: 0 em-dash lines.
Vague placeholder usage was reduced to two intentional instances. Verified count after revision: 2 occurrences of 'something'.
Habitual 'the way' constructions were removed across the chapter. Verified count after revision: 0 occurrences.

finding_results.notes: Roughened the Kai-Yusuf confrontation across all three cited spans. Yusuf's Farah explanation now trails off mid-sentence before Kai interrupts (161-163). The virus analysis delivery stumbles twice: Yusuf loses his thread at 179 ('And it's —'), Kai cuts in with a one-word prompt at 181, and Yusuf's conclusion fumbles at 187 ('I can't give you the meeting where they — I can't give you that'). The leash thesis arrives through faster, less controlled speech at 193 rather than as a prepared briefing. The prognosis delivery breaks when Yusuf stops mid-sentence at the spinal compression detail (213) and the glasses-on-off gesture replaces clinical composure at 217. Kai's moral confrontation now arrives through body (wrist and shoulder ache at 225) and gesture (pointing at monitors) rather than a three-part rhetorical escalation. His closing line at 229 is rougher and less quotable than the original. Narrator analysis trimmed at 199 and narrator thesis simplified at 211 to reduce composed interpretive work. All three required disclosures still land clearly: archive theft, Meridian-signature suspicion, and Tomas's prognosis. Birmingham register fractures Yusuf's professional exposition at three points (165, 193, 213) instead of arriving only in the prognosis.

Recast Kai's late-scene interiority into visible detail and plain judgment: Yusuf's cold tea and shaking hands, the yellow monitor blocks, and the calibration rigs waiting under fluorescent light. Removed the more essayistic trust/apparatus phrasing.

pass_summaries.summary: Fixed all packet findings in chapter 14 by restoring blackout continuity, earning Kai's archive discovery through incident-mode access and explicit illicit-routing cues, replacing the Yusuf example-line echo, removing repeated reteaching, and reducing chapter-level recurrence counts to within target.

Revised the Kai-Yusuf confrontation to break the pattern of consecutive polished mini-speeches. Three major disclosures are now interrupted or verbally fumbled under pressure: Yusuf's Farah explanation trails off and gets cut by Kai, the virus analysis arrives through stumbles and restarts rather than clean briefing, and the prognosis delivery breaks twice mid-sentence. Kai's anger now arrives through body intrusion and gesture rather than composed rhetorical escalation. Yusuf's Birmingham register fractures his professional mode earlier and more often. Narrator analysis paragraphs trimmed to reduce composed thesis-work. All required information still lands. No changes to the non-dialogue scenes (Tomas's blackout, Margot's feed experience, Kai's archive discovery).

Revised chapter 14 in place for the prose/copyedit pass while preserving the chapter's existing structure and boundary beats. Kai's POV now stays concrete and bodily in the late confrontation, and the repeated negation/correction emphasis pattern has been reduced to a single deliberate use in Tomas's blackout.
